title: Insect bites/stings (starter evidence summary)
text:
Telephone triage for bites/stings focuses on allergic reactions, infection, and tick-borne illness signs.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Trouble breathing, fainting, or swelling of lips/tongue/face after sting (possible anaphylaxis)

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Rapidly spreading redness/warmth/pain (possible cellulitis)
- Fever or feeling very ill after bite
- Tick bite with expanding rash or flu-like illness

Supportive care (mild local reaction):
- Cold compress, avoid scratching; OTC symptom relief if safe.
- Seek in-person care if worsening swelling, spreading redness, or systemic symptoms develop.
